# Quick Start

## Installation

<Tabs>
  <TabItem value="npm" label="npm" default>

```bash
npm install @warlock.js/cache
```

  </TabItem>
  <TabItem value="yarn" label="yarn">

```bash
yarn add @warlock.js/cache
```

  </TabItem>
  <TabItem value="pnpm" label="pnpm">

```bash
pnpm add @warlock.js/cache
```

  </TabItem>
</Tabs>

## Setup

```ts
import { cache, MemoryCacheDriver, CACHE_FOR } from "@warlock.js/cache";

cache.setCacheConfigurations({
  default: "memory",
  drivers: { memory: MemoryCacheDriver },
  options: { memory: { ttl: CACHE_FOR.ONE_HOUR } },
});
await cache.init();
```

## Core Patterns

### set / get

Store and retrieve data:

```ts
await cache.set(`products.${id}`, product, CACHE_FOR.ONE_HOUR);
const product = await cache.get(`products.${id}`); // null if expired or missing
```

### remember

Fetch from cache or compute on miss:

```ts
const categoryProducts = await cache.remember(
  `products.category.${categoryId}`,
  CACHE_FOR.HALF_HOUR,
  () => db.products.findByCategory(categoryId), // only called on cache miss
);
```

### removeNamespace

Invalidate all keys in a namespace (e.g. after checkout):

```ts
await cache.removeNamespace(`orders.${userId}`);
```

## Key Concepts

- **TTL**: Time-to-live in seconds (how long values stay cached before expiring)
- **Dot-notation keys**: Use hierarchical keys like `"products.123"` or `"category.electronics.featured"` to organize related cache entries
- **Namespaces**: Groups of related keys that can be invalidated together with `removeNamespace()`
